title: Warmly Sauteed Canadian Oyster Mushrooms Skillet
description: A light Canadian dish featuring oyster mushrooms, this recipe is a perfect combination of flavors and textures, with a jammy and bouncy qq finish.
instructions: Rinse the oyster mushrooms and submerge them in an ice bath to shock them and help retain their texture.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and add the olive oil.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chopped onion and cook for 3 minutes until translucent.
Remove the mushrooms from the ice bath and add them to the skillet, cooking for 5 minutes until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Add the white wine and chicken broth to the skillet and stir to deglaze the bottom.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes until the liquid has reduced and the mushrooms are tender.
Stir in the dried thyme, salt, and pepper, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the mushroom mixture hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
